tweets.text:
Leading the way! 

Did you know @USBPChiefDTM has the most Border Patrol drug seizures along the Northern Border?

From fiscal year 2019 through March 31, 2026, Detroit Sector recorded 681 narcotics seizures — more than any other U.S. Border Patrol Northern Border sector. Detroit sector covers the entirety of the state of Michigan as well as Ohio. 

More: https://t.co/IjC7HklLVw.

tweets.text:
As the United States continues to prepare for this summer’s FIFA World Cup 2026™, CBP is there to ensure a safe, successful, and welcoming tournament, which will be the largest and most complex sporting event in world history.

From Oct. 1, 2025, through April 30, 2026, CBP received over 5.9 million ESTA applications under the Visa Waiver Program, resulting in over 5 million approvals. The top five nations for approvals were the United Kingdom with over 1.2 million, followed by France (over 570,000), Germany (over 530,000), Japan (over 500,000), and the Republic of Korea (over 320,000).

Read more about our efforts here: https://t.co/TLJmLFSVKz

tweets.text:
🚨BIG SEIZURE AT JFK! 🚨 

This wasn’t just a violation…it was a threat to our communities. CBP officers uncovered a falsely declared suppressor, leading to the discovery of firearms, high-capacity magazines, body armor, narcotics, and explosive material. Every seizure is a reminder: vigilance at our ports of entry is critical to keeping our communities safe. Read: https://t.co/c7Omfa9OqC

tweets.text:
Blue Nitro stopped in Philadelphia!

Two packages from Spain labeled as "skincare" were analyzed and found to contain 2.5 quarts of GBL.

GBL is an industrial solvent used as a drug that can cause: unconsciousness, seizures, slowed heart rate, severe respiratory depression, decreased body temperature, vomiting, nausea, coma, or death. 
an

Read more: https://t.co/3AkaiDGWOa

Foot Guide Arrested!

Another illegal alien who called 911 with a medical emergency in the desert at an earlier date had been abandoned by the illegal alien pictured here, who admitted to serving as a foot guide.

Our Yuma Sector agents rescued the endangered illegal alien, and now this criminal will face the consequences of his negligence. 

A reminder that human smugglers do not care about their "customers"!

tweets.text:
At the San Ysidro Port of Entry, our officers seized more than 60 pounds of cocaine worth more than $1.15 million concealed within a vehicle driven by a trusted traveler.

The driver was arrested and faces federal prosecution. 

We will hold everyone accountable for their actions, especially those who betray the trust of our traveler programs by attempting to smuggle dangerous narcotics.

https://t.co/eGEWfNJdUa

We will hold everyone accountable for their actions, especially those who betray the trust of our traveler programs by attempting to smuggle dangerous narcotics.

https://go.dhs.gov/ivv

Counterfeit consumer goods may also be sourced or manufactured in facilities that employ forced labor.

tweets.text:
CBP Officers in Atlanta have seized hundreds of thousands of dollars of outbound unreported cash so far this year. In each seizure, CBP officers recited the currency report law and verified that the travelers understood. 

Travelers can travel with any amount of currency and other monetary instruments that they choose, but any amounts over $10,000 must be reported to the U.S. Treasury. During the first three months of 2026, CBP officers helped travelers complete the required forms that totaled nearly $3 million.

Detector dogs like "Pub" play a crucial role in this work. 

Read more ➡️ https://t.co/5jmLOXmDe1

tweets.text:
CBP Mogs Smugglers!

 A shipper in China attempted to clandestinely introduce various types of peptides, particularly the active ingredients for Glucagon-like peptide-1, or GLP-1, weight loss drugs into the United States.

Since discovering this scheme, Cincinnati CBP officers identified over 300 master carton smuggling attempts. Each carton concealed approximately 15 unmanifested, prelabeled shipments for a total of about 5,000 individual shipments of peptides.

These unapproved peptides are manufactured overseas in facilities that may not maintain standards within their manufacturing lines or labs, and could be contaminated with other substances, deadly bacteria, or other impurities.

Don't be a jester, ensure that you use FDA approved medicine.

Read more ➡️https://t.co/AT5XdlldT8

tweets.text:
More illegal aliens stopped by @CBPAMO!

23 arrested in failed human smuggling attempt off the California Coast! On Saturday, DHC-8 and Multi-Role Enforcement Aircraft aircrews coordinated with a Coastal Interceptor Vessel Crew to intercept a panga-style vessel west of San Clemente Island.

After maintaining visual contact and vectoring support from the air, Marine Interdiction Agents successfully interdicted the vessel with 23 illegal aliens onboard just after 1:00 p.m. Air and Marine Operations seized the vessel, and all subjects were turned over to San Diego ICE ERO. Excellent teamwork and coordination from the air and sea ensured a safe and effective operation! #BorderSecurity
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Another excellent seizure by West Palm Beach’s Trade Enforcement Team, with assistance from the Center of Excellence and Expertise Apparel, Footwear and Textiles team, West Palm CBP Research and Intelligence Operations Team (RIOT), and Homeland Security Investigations (HSI). 

The operation resulted in the arrest of an individual on felony charges for possessing and intending to distribute over 25 counterfeit items. Additionally, two illegal aliens were identified by officers and transported to U.S. Border Patrol West Palm Beach Station for processing.

The estimated Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) of the seized counterfeit merchandise, had it been genuine, exceeds $1,500,000.00 USD. Distribution of counterfeit products is not a victimless crime, CBP is committed to protecting the American public from dangerous and illicit goods.

tweets.text:
🚨 Securing Our Border: The "Puebla Tree" Gap is CLOSED! 🚨

For years, the Otay Mountain Wilderness in San Diego, California, was plagued by dangerous cartel enforcers and bandits operating near the "Puebla Tree" border gap. As recently as 2024, these criminal organizations patrolled near the international border, ensuring cartel "plaza" payments were made before crossing. They exploited these easily traversable gaps to cross into the U.S., where they would rob, kidnap, and extort anyone they encountered.

Today, that has changed. Thanks to the relentless efforts of our @USBPChiefSDC agents, our partnerships, and the strategic infrastructure improvements, the "Puebla Tree" border gap is now CLOSED. We've sent a clear message: these criminal activities are no longer tolerated, and cartels are not welcome here. Our commitment to securing our communities and protecting those along the border remains unwavering.

tweets.text:
VEHICLE STOP ➡️ DEADLY DRUGS  

Border Patrol and the Orange County Sheriff's Office stopped a vehicle in San Clemente, leading to the seizure of $250K worth of fentanyl. We found 9 bundles weighing 22 lbs. concealed inside the center console. The driver was arrested and booked into the local jail. CBP and our local law enforcement work together to keep fentanyl off our streets.

tweets.text:
CBP agriculture specialists in Detroit intercepted an invasive grain pest that is quarantine significant. Thanks to specialized training and vigilance, a traveler was referred to secondary inspection when seeds for growing purposes were found in their luggage. Inside the bag was a single cast skin, confirmed by the USDA as belonging to the khapra beetle.  Our team is frontline safeguarding our economy and environment from dangerous pests and diseases.

Read more: https://t.co/2MbmR8T8vT

tweets.text:
Our “Se Busca Información” initiative has new suspects, and we need your help to find them. You can confidentially report any information via WhatsApp or by calling 830-313-4557. Our phone line is open 24 hours, and calls go directly to the Border Patrol’s Texas call center. We share information with law enforcement in the U.S. and Mexico. The bilateral initiative between the U.S. and Mexico is in best interest of both countries to promote a safer and more secure shared border region.

Learn more: https://t.co/uCHsaGvJDV

tweets.text:
This holiday season, keep the family together while going through customs with Mobile Passport Control. Now one family member can submit the entire family's travel information through the app and you can speak to a CBP officer together.

Yes, it's free! 

The benefits of MPC are:
- Shorter wait times in Customs
- Free, easy-to-use app
- Groups up to 12 can be processed together
- Ability to declare items in app

More: https://t.co/Gfo9sHU6dT

tweets.text:
A quick tidbit before you travel this holiday season:
Marijuana is still a controlled substance under U.S. federal law, which means that selling, possessing, producing, or distributing both medical and recreational cannabis is illegal! If you're thinking about crossing the border with marijuana, you should reconsider. Doing so could lead to serious consequences such as seizure, hefty fines, or even arrest! Plus, it might affect your ability to enter the U.S.  #KnowBeforeYouGo 💼✈️

Check out more info here: https://t.co/BkqmLORmMd

tweets.text:
Operators of small pleasure vessels, arriving in the United States, including Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands, from a foreign port or place must, by law, report their arrival to CBP immediately. 

Make it easy by using the ROAM app.  
Travelers can input their biographic, conveyance, and trip details, and submit the information to be reviewed by a CBP Officer.

Read more reporting requirements in the Caribbean➡️https://t.co/KCBTBhhqTJ
Learn more about the ROAM app here ➡️ https://t.co/cSk87nG5G6

tweets.text:
Way to bury the lede! Earlier today, Border Patrol removed two violent criminals from a Chicago suburb school.

Agents were pursuing the two Mexican nationals as part of Operation Midway Blitz, when they fled into the school.

It’s a great thing our agents caught them.

Josafat Garcia-Roa: Multiple DUIs, felony cocaine possession, multiple domestic batteries and assaults, violation of bail bond.

Samuel Suarez-Cuevas: Kidnapping, multiple counts of domestic violence and domestic battery, criminal damage. 

CBP is committed to removing criminal illegal aliens from our country.

One Mission, North and South.

tweets.text:
Border crossing update! Starting tomorrow September 30, 2025, Customs and Border Protection will increase the CBP I-94 non-immigrant form fee from $6 to $30 dollars.

The I-94 fee had remained stagnant for years until the passage of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. This overdue monetary increase will surge revenue generated by CBP at no cost to the American public.
  
For more info, check the federal register: https://t.co/NOZiyj7P7w

tweets.text:
🚨 Attention, travelers! Did you know that marijuana is still a controlled substance under U.S. federal law? This means that selling, possessing, producing, or distributing both medical and recreational cannabis is illegal! Think twice before crossing the border with marijuana. Doing so could lead to serious consequences like seizure, hefty fines, or even arrest! Plus, it might affect your ability to enter the U.S. Stay informed and safe and #KnowBeforeYouGo 💼✈️

Check out more info here: https://t.co/BkqmLORmMd

tweets.text:
Oklahoma CBP officers found a Byzantine-era ceramic artifact during a shipment inspection. Working alongside @HSI_HQ and University of Oklahoma scholars, it was confirmed that the artifact is authentic and may have been a hand grenade. This incredible find highlights CBP's dedication to protecting cultural property from illicit markets. 

Thanks to the vigilance of our officers, this artifact is in safe hands at the Sam Noble Oklahoma Museum of Natural History, where scholars will conserve, research, and interpret the artifact's history for public education.

Read more ➡️ https://t.co/8SUBMCPGXZ

tweets.text:
During a routine inspection at @Dulles_Airport CBP Port officers encountered an individual with suspicious evidence. This evidence was linked to an American man who was the director and pastor of a church orphanage in Pampanga, Philippines accused of child abuse. Officers reported their findings to U.S. federal authorities and Filipino authorities leading to an investigation. 
 
Thanks to the work of @DFOBaltimore officers, Filipino authorities closed the facility, and 156 children were rescued from unsafe conditions. Through global partnerships, we can ensure those with malicious intent will face consequences.

tweets.text:
Through our strong partnership with @HSINewYork and the @EDNYnews, a prolific trafficker of ancient Egyptian artifacts has been sentenced to six months in prison. In January 2020, a traveler arriving from Egypt to JFK airport falsely declared the value of his goods at only $300. An inspection resulted in the recovery of 590 artifacts and materials used to fabricate documents. CBP is committed to enforcing the law and protecting the priceless heritage of human civilization from exploitation. We stand vigilant in safeguarding history for generations to come.

Read more ➡️ https://t.co/8Ei8ArnrRu

tweets.text:
We are taking action to stop the trafficking of firearms across our borders. A Trinidad and Tobago national has been sentenced to five years in federal prison for conspiring to smuggle firearms from the U.S. to Trinidad and Tobago. This individual, along with co-conspirators, attempted to ship a variety of firearms and ammunition in two punching bags labeled as "household items." 

CBP continues to work with @HSI_HQ, @ATFHQ, @USCIS, and both domestic and international law enforcement partners to identify, disrupt, and dismantle transnational criminal organizations. We will stop at nothing to go after the bad guys.

Read more ➡️ https://t.co/YZ5zHBv7Ky

tweets.text:
Take advantage of these CBP apps.

The CBP Home App gives an opportunity for illegal aliens to receive cost-free travel, forgiveness of any failure to depart fines, and a $1,000 exit bonus to support travel back to their home country or another country where they have lawful status.

The CBP Link App streamlines travel-related processes and reduces wait times.

Enhanced Passenger Processing in airports using facial biometrics and RFID technology expedites entry for U.S. citizens and trusted travelers, improving efficiency and enforcement.

tweets.text:
An important part of the Facing Fentanyl event is remembering the lives lost due to this drug and other drugs. The Black Project Memorial Program, held at the iconic St. Patrick's Cathedral in NYC, is a time for the families and all those affected to come together and reflect. 
CBP Chaplain Lilian Santiago delivers a message of comfort. She states, “Entering ‘America’s Parish Church' and gazing upon every face, reading each name and tearfully reflecting on these precious lives taken from us too soon can only serve to strengthen our resolve in making sure that each loss will not be in vain."

It's January 17, 1950 at the Laredo Bridge System in Texas.
Produce from Mexico is being transferred to American trucks and manufactured items such as straw hats and raw items are being processed through U.S. Customs. 
Just another day at the border, inspecting and protecting.

Photo by T. W. Kines

#ThrowbackThursday

tweets.text:
Take advantage of the technological advances made within CBP.

The CBP Home App gives an opportunity for illegal aliens to receive cost-free travel, forgiveness of any failure to depart fines, and a $1,000 exit bonus to support travel back to their home country or another country where they have lawful status.

The CBP Link App streamlines travel-related processes and reduces wait times.

Enhanced Passenger Processing in airports using facial biometrics and RFID technology expedites entry for U.S. citizens and trusted travelers, improving efficiency and enforcement.

tweets.text:
Stopping tiny threats. 

In April, @DFOSanJuan CBP Ag specialist Garcia intercepted a beetle of the Bostrychoplites genus at the cargo facility in Puerto Rico. Hidden within wood packaging in a shipment, this destructive pest was identified by his expertise and sharp eye. This marks the first-ever interception of this species in Puerto Rico, underscoring CBP's critical role in safeguarding our nation's agriculture and ecosystems.

tweets.text:
Some history for you on this Throwback Thursday:
The historic United States Courthouse and Federal Building in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, originally served as the U.S. Post Office, Court, and Customs House. Completed in 1892 after seven years of construction, this five-story landmark was home to postal services, the courts, and officials who oversaw import and export functions. Located just three blocks from Lake Michigan in downtown Milwaukee, it was once home to U.S. Customs Service (now CBP). An architectural "Crown Jewel," the building was designated a Milwaukee City Landmark in 1972.
The more you know.
#TBT #CBP #History

tweets.text: The original U.S. Customs Ensign was designed by Treasury Secretary Oliver Wolcott in 1799, 10 years after the founding of U.S. Customs Service. It was the first flag designed specifically for an agency of the federal government. Today, a modified version of the flag is used by CBP. On the 236th anniversary of the U.S. Customs Service, we remember our legacy.

"To fight the bug, we must understand the bug."

CBP agriculture specialists in Detroit found fresh cherries infested with European cherry fruit fly maggots and larvae. The traveler who had the infested cherries was headed to Door County, Wisconsin, a major cherry growing area. If CBP hadn't caught them, the fruit flies could have devastated orchards, hurt family farms, driven up prices, and required serious mitigation efforts.

Read more here ➡️: https://t.co/2wXPDueqz6
